corrections_by_claim.note: The article mentions R&D spending increases, but this is a national aggregate, not a firm-level analysis of productive investment vs. rent-seeking. It fails the mechanism gate.
No engagement with the specific profit mechanisms of search or data.

The article describes state industrial policy (planning, funding), but it does not engage with the theoretical claim that the state is 'constitutive' of tech dominance as a counter to the 'parcelized sovereignty' of techno-feudalism. It is a descriptive account of state-led capitalism, not a theoretical test of the mode of production.
No discussion of profit rates or the nature of surplus value.
No engagement with the meta-theoretical debate on expropriation.
